evidence_excerpt: The patients died at ages 8 and 7, respectively.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Followed by progressive deterioration of gait and mental functioning with gradual development of ataxia and spasticity; EEGs showed active generalized epileptic discharges with severe generalized disturbance of cerebral activity.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Progressive deterioration of gait and mental functioning with gradual development of ataxia and spasticity.
The younger sibling… had lost the ability to speak by 3–4 years.
By the age of 5 years, the older boy had no comprehensible speech… markedly ataxic gait, with spasticity in all four limbs.
EEGs showed active generalized epileptic discharges with severe generalized disturbance of cerebral activity.
The patients died at ages 8 and 7, respectively.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact diagnosis date is not stated; a preliminary diagnosis was made by brain biopsy and later confirmed by serum hexosaminidase assays. Timing of EEG findings and progression relative to diagnosis is not precisely documented; mid- vs long-term categorizations are inferred from ages (onset ~18–24 months, speech loss by 3–5 years, death at 7–8 years). The record describes two siblings; outcomes reflect the shared clinical course.
